reasons: Karrie's max-HP true damage directly pressures Belerick's tank identity.
She is one of the clearest front-to-back answers when protected.

proof.summary: Belerick wants to win through high HP and return-damage punishment, while Karrie converts repeated hits into max-HP true damage that threatens tanks.
worksBestWhen: Karrie kites outside Belerick's taunt range.

reasons: Lunox can pressure durable frontliners without relying only on basic-attack trades.
She can use defensive timing to avoid Belerick's punish window.

proof.summary: Belerick wants enemies to hit him repeatedly in punishable range, while Lunox can pressure him with magic burst cycles and defensive timing.
failureCases: Lunox wastes defensive mode before committing damage.

reasons: Dyrroth can reduce Belerick's physical defense and burst him down with focused follow-up.
Compared with Alpha, Dyrroth is more direct and front-loaded into tank durability.

proof.summary: Belerick wants to survive through durability and return damage, while Dyrroth can reduce physical defense and create a focused burst window against him.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth lands Spectre Step or Fatal Strike before committing ultimate.
failureCases: Dyrroth is taunted before landing defense reduction.
Dyrroth commits alone into multiple enemies.

reasons: X.Borg can pressure Belerick with sustained damage and true-damage threat.
His armor state gives more safety than fragile damage dealers when fighting near Belerick.

proof.summary: Belerick wants close-range punish trades, while X.Borg can poke and threaten true damage through his damage pattern.
worksBestWhen: X.Borg avoids being taunted before armor or detonation timing.
failureCases: X.Borg dives too deep into Belerick's team.
Belerick's team bursts X.Borg after armor breaks.

reasons: Alpha has true damage through Beta and can sustain in extended fights.
He is valid, but slightly below Dyrroth because Belerick can punish extended repeated-hit fights more easily than front-loaded defense-reduction burst.

proof.summary: Belerick wants to absorb damage and punish attackers, while Alpha can repeatedly trigger Beta true damage and sustain through frontline fighting.
worksBestWhen: Alpha has enough sustain and item timing to survive Belerick's control.
Alpha fights with follow-up instead of dueling into full enemy backup.
failureCases: Belerick taunts Alpha before Alpha finishes his combo.
Alpha is forced into a long fight while Belerick's team follows up.
Alpha's sustain is reduced by anti-heal or burst focus.
